ai-w7-t1.answer: AI drafts SQL from plain English. Always review: correct tables, JOINs, WHERE clauses, and use **parameterized** queries — never paste user input into strings.
ai-w7-t1.realWorld: Ask: 'SELECT students with grade > 80, JOIN courses.' Run in local DB; fix wrong column names AI invented.

ai-w7-t2.answer: Describe entities and relationships; AI suggests schema. You validate keys, types, and normalization before CREATE TABLE.
ai-w7-t2.realWorld: Library system: Book, Member, Loan — AI forgets FK on member_id; you add it.

ai-w7-t3.answer: AI can sketch ER diagrams from requirements. Check cardinality: one-to-many vs many-to-many needs junction table.
ai-w7-t3.realWorld: Student *—* Course requires enrollment table — AI sometimes draws direct many-to-many without it.

ai-w7-t4.answer: Paste slow query + EXPLAIN output. Ask what to index or rewrite. AI guesses; EXPLAIN proves.
ai-w7-t4.realWorld: Full table scan on users.email — add index after verifying selectivity.

ai-w7-t5.answer: EXPLAIN shows scan type, rows examined, keys used. Ask AI to translate jargon: 'type=ALL means bad for big tables.'
ai-w7-t5.realWorld: rows: 500000, type: ALL → add WHERE index or rewrite JOIN order.

ai-w7-t6.answer: CRUD prompts: specify table, columns, types. Generate INSERT/UPDATE with ? placeholders for JDBC.
ai-w7-t6.realWorld: INSERT INTO student (name, roll) VALUES (?, ?) — PreparedStatement pattern.

ai-w7-t7.answer: AI lists JUnit cases for DAO methods: null params, empty result, duplicate key. You write assertions.
ai-w7-t7.realWorld: findById(-1) should return empty Optional, not throw — define behavior first.

ai-w7-t8.answer: JDBC errors: SQLException message, SQL state, line. Paste to AI with your connection URL (redact password).
ai-w7-t8.realWorld: Connection refused → wrong port; Syntax error → AI fixes SQL dialect mismatch.

ai-w7-t9.answer: Generate table/column comments and schema README. Edit for accuracy — AI doesn't know your naming conventions.
ai-w7-t9.realWorld: Schema doc for onboarding — AI first draft, you fix table relationships.

ai-w7-t10.answer: AI SQL risks: missing indexes, wrong dialect (MySQL vs Postgres), SQL injection in generated examples. Never run unreviewed SQL on prod.
ai-w7-t10.realWorld: AI writes SELECT * WHERE id = " + userId — reject; use PreparedStatement.
